steps.title: Read two points from the line of best fit
steps.content: Read the $y$-intercept of the line of best fit for data set A from the graph. It is about $53.75$.

steps.title: Find the slope and original y-intercept
steps.content: Data set B is created by subtracting $11$ from every $y$-value in data set A. That shifts the entire line of best fit down $11$ units, so the $y$-intercept also decreases by $11$.

steps.title: Apply the vertical shift of $-11$
steps.content: The shifted intercept is approximately $$53.75-11=42.75.$$ That confirms C is correct.
